title: macOS Notebook Battery Health and Cycle Count Threshold
dataSources: `index=os` scripted metrics from `pmset -g batt`, `ioreg -rn AppleSmartBattery` (cycle count, max capacity %, AC/watts)
app: Splunk Universal Forwarder for macOS, Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (`Splunk_TA_nix`, Splunkbase #833) for optional scripted input packaging
spl:
index=os sourcetype=macos_battery host=*
| stats latest(cycle_count) as cycles, latest(max_capacity_pct) as health_pct, latest(battery_condition) as condition by host
| where cycles > 1000 OR health_pct < 80 OR like(condition, "%Service%")
description: Lithium battery wear on Mac notebooks shows up as rising cycle counts, falling maximum capacity versus design, and "Service Recommended" conditions from power management. Tracking these signals prioritizes depot battery swaps before users lose runtime or swell-risk events ground a fleet.
value: Reduces unexpected power loss and field failures by surfacing degraded Mac batteries before runtime collapses or Apple flags a service condition.
knownFalsePositives: Loaner laptops and lab units can sit at high cycle counts for years on AC without field risk. `ioreg` values jump after SMC reset or macOS updates—confirm with a second sample before procurement tickets. TCC/privacy prompts can block `ioreg` in some user sessions; the script may return partial fields that look worse than reality.
implementation: Schedule a lightweight scripted input (UF `inputs.conf`) every 6–24h: parse `pmset -g batt` for state and `ioreg -rn AppleSmartBattery` for `CycleCount`, `AppleRawMaxCapacity` vs `DesignCapacity`, and `PermanentFailureStatus`. Normalize `max_capacity_pct=100*AppleRawMaxCapacity/DesignCapacity`. Set `battery_condition` from `pmset` or ioreg `Condition` string. Route to `index=os`, `sourcetype=macos_battery`. Alert when cycles exceed policy (often 800–1200 for corporate refresh), health under 80%, or condition indicates service.
visualization: Single value (hosts below health threshold), Table (host, cycles, health_pct, condition), Histogram (cycle count distribution).

references.title: Splunk Documentation: Install the universal forwarder on macOS
Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (Splunkbase 833)

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Universal Forwarder on macOS with a `bin/` script scheduled via `inputs.conf`.
• Python or shell access to `pmset` and `ioreg` (no root required for read-only battery queries in most builds).

Step 1 — Emit one JSON or key=value event per host per run with fields: `cycle_count`, `max_capacity_pct`, `battery_condition`, optional `is_charging`.

Step 2 — Save the SPL as a scheduled alert with a lookup allowlist for loaner/demo devices.

Step 3 — Validate against Apple System Information → Power sample values.

Step 4 — Operationalize with asset tag join on `host` for procurement.
grandmaExplanation: We watch how worn down laptop batteries are getting so support can replace them before people suddenly run out of juice in the middle of work.
